mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, equity risk premiums expand as real borrowing costs rise, compressing forward valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite elevated FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following PAYEMS declines are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.05), or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and SPX 60-day returns is < 0.10 in absolute value.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, consumer demand destruction signals earnings headwinds that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The joint deterioration amplifies the signal beyond either indicator alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683974 million and UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2022-09-01
regime_hint: late-cycle contraction with simultaneous labor and demand weakness
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS decline × UNRATE rise) produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window, or SPX returns following joint triggers are not significantly more negative than single-indicator triggers (ANCOVA p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and inflation persistence, which under elevated monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 2%) triggers equity multiple compression and negative 5-day forward returns as real yields rise.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5464 and FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of 2022-09-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening with strong supply-side activity
failure_condition: Joint condition (INDPRO > 1% MoM AND FEDFUNDS > 2%) fails to predict negative 5-day returns with Rank IC > -0.08, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1400 thousand units annualized, signaling construction sector contraction and reduced forward demand for materials and labor, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations reset downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1575 thousand units as of 2022-08-01
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns in periods when HOUST < 1400 are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises > 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains below the prior month's level, monetary policy is effectively loosening in real terms, signaling future rate hikes and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors front-run the policy cycle.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged or lower MoM
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 296.761 and FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of 2022-09-01
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL rise × FEDFUNDS lag) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines below 3.6% signals tight labor markets and wage pressure, which under elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 295) triggers expectations of sustained monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as terminal rate expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% and CPIAUCSL at 296.761 as of 2022-09-01
regime_hint: tight labor market with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Joint condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns with Rank IC > -0.06, or SPX returns following tight labor + high inflation triggers are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown and reduced corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations compress and equity risk premiums expand.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19895.271 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2022-04-01
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during periods of sub-1% GDPC1 growth are not significantly negative (mean return > 0% with t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
